articles.headline: TMC faces potential split as rumblings over LoP position intensify
articles.summary: The Trinamool Congress (TMC) in West Bengal faces a crisis as nearly 60 dissident legislators allegedly support expelled MLA Ritabrata Banerjee as the Leader of the Opposition. Mamata Banerjee has accused the BJP of attempting to split the party by pressuring its MLAs, citing similar political events that occurred in Maharashtra.

articles.headline: Supreme Court sets 5-member panel to define Aravalli range
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has constituted a five-member committee to provide a uniform definition of the ecologically fragile Aravalli range. The panel is tasked with submitting a report by August 31 to help regulate future mining activities and ensure the conservation of the range across different states.

articles.headline: Buzz over Annamalai resignation as he meets Shah and Nabin
articles.summary: Speculation regarding K Annamalai's resignation as Tamil Nadu BJP chief grew after he met Union Home Minister Amit Shah and party leader Nitin Nabin. Annamalai reportedly wants to leave the state unit to start his own outfit. Party leaders indicated he may be given a free hand or a central role if he continues with the BJP.

articles.headline: DK Shivakumar to be sworn in today with 13 ministers
articles.summary: Congress leader DK Shivakumar will take oath as Karnataka’s new chief minister today, along with approximately 13 other ministers. Senior party leaders, including Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi, are expected to attend the ceremony at Lok Bhavan in Bengaluru. The list of ministers includes G Parameshwara and Priyank Kharge.

articles.headline: Delhi airport's third runway to remain shut until September
articles.summary: The third runway at Delhi's Indira Gandhi International Airport will remain closed until September for essential strengthening and upgrades. Originally planned to reopen in July, the delay is due to the addition of key systems intended to increase aircraft-handling capacity by 10%. Officials stated the extension aims to avoid future shutdowns.

articles.headline: Security stepped up in Bengaluru for CM swearing-in ceremony
articles.summary: Bengaluru police have deployed extensive security at Lok Bhavan and surrounding areas for the swearing-in of DK Shivakumar as CM. The premises have been divided into five sectors with 4,000 personnel. Traffic diversions are in place as thousands of supporters are expected to arrive from across Karnataka.

articles.headline: Ex-MLC abducted and freed after ransom demand in Bengaluru
articles.summary: A former member of the Karnataka Legislative Council was allegedly abducted by an armed group from the outskirts of Bengaluru. The victim was released after several hours of negotiations and a ransom payment. Police have registered an FIR and formed special teams to apprehend the suspects who fled in a vehicle with a Kerala registration.

articles.headline: Former minister booked by CBI in Valmiki corporation fraud case
articles.summary: The CBI has filed a charge sheet against former Karnataka minister B Nagendra and 28 others in the ₹89.63 crore Valmiki Scheduled Tribes Development Corporation scam. The agency alleges that funds were diverted through forged documents and a complex web of bank accounts. Nagendra had resigned earlier this year following the allegations.

articles.headline: Telangana releases 91 convicts under special remission policy
articles.summary: The Telangana government has facilitated the release of 91 convicts from various prisons under a special remission policy. The group includes 85 men and six women who were selected based on good behavior. Officials emphasized the goal of social reintegration, giving the released individuals a second chance to lead productive lives.

articles.headline: TN and Centre sign Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0 agreement
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu government and the Union Jal Shakti Ministry have signed an MoU for Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0. The project, valued at ₹18,123 crore, aims to provide functional tap connections to every rural household by 2028. The first phase of the extension will receive ₹2,177.27 crore in immediate funding.

articles.headline: Delhi HC allows multi-way organ swap transplants beyond two pairs
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has ruled that multi-way organ swap transplants involving more than two pairs are permissible under the Transplantation of Human Organs and Tissues Act. The court clarified that the law does not restrict swaps to only two donor-recipient pairs, provided they meet non-commerciality and compatibility requirements.

articles.headline: Tigress Zeenat gives birth to four cubs in Odisha reserve
articles.summary: A tigress named Zeenat has given birth to four cubs at the Similipal Tiger Reserve in Odisha. Zeenat was translocated from Maharashtra in 2024 to improve the genetic diversity of the tiger population in Similipal. The birth is seen as a significant success for the state's wildlife conservation and translocation efforts.

articles.headline: Lakshadweep admin plans beachfront land acquisition amid green concerns
articles.summary: The Lakshadweep administration plans to acquire 121,359 sq m of beachfront land on Agatti island for a beach peripheral road. The move has raised concerns among locals regarding the environmental impact on coral reefs and their traditional livelihoods. A social impact assessment is being conducted to address displacement and compensation issues.

articles.headline: ASI plans to restore Taj Mahal mosque's western flank
articles.summary: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has prepared a detailed conservation plan to restore the mosque on the western flank of the Taj Mahal complex. The project, awaiting formal approval and funding, aims to repair structural issues and preserve the historic fabric of the monument, which last saw major conservation work a decade ago.

articles.headline: Three cloudbursts in J&K trigger flash floods and blockades
articles.summary: Heavy rains and three separate cloudbursts in Jammu and Kashmir's Kishtwar and Doda districts have triggered flash floods and road blockades. Union Minister Jitendra Singh stated that emergency teams have been deployed. No loss of life has been reported, but several vehicles were trapped under debris, and schools in affected areas were shut.

articles.headline: Israel hits Lebanon despite Trump call for de-escalation
articles.summary: Israeli drone strikes targeted southern Lebanon on Tuesday, following US President-elect Donald Trump's call for a ceasefire. Hezbollah retaliated with drones and rockets. The ongoing hostilities, which intensified in April, have caused significant civilian displacement. Negotiations are currently viewed as the most viable path to end the conflict.

articles.headline: India included in wider rollout of Anthropic's Mythos AI model
articles.summary: Anthropic is expanding the rollout of its Mythos AI model to over 15 countries, including India. The move follows a trial period where the model's safety and capability to identify computer security weaknesses were tested. Major tech firms like Google and Amazon are among the partners for this expanded deployment of the advanced AI tool.

articles.headline: Argentina prays for one more World Cup from Lionel Messi
articles.summary: As the FIFA World Cup approaches, fans in Argentina are hoping for a final masterclass from 38-year-old Lionel Messi. Despite his age and recent injuries at Inter Miami, Messi remains the central figure of the national team. Manager Lionel Scaloni continues to build the squad around him for the tournament in the USA, Canada, and Mexico.

articles.headline: DK Shivakumar to open CM stint with 8 populist schemes
articles.summary: DK Shivakumar is set to announce eight populist schemes focused on farmers and workers after taking oath as Karnataka CM at 4:10pm today. Around 15 ministers are expected to be sworn in alongside him. Outgoing CM Siddaramaiah has been appointed to the Congress Working Committee, marking his transition to a national role.

articles.headline: TMC firefights fears of 50-MLA exodus after BJP claims
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress is managing internal turmoil after a BJP minister claimed about 50 TMC legislators are ready to defect. The situation echoes the recent split in the Shiv Sena, as TMC leadership attempts to maintain party unity amid rumors of a mass exodus to the opposition.

articles.headline: Former TN BJP chief Annamalai resigns, summoned to Delhi
articles.summary: K. Annamalai has resigned as the Tamil Nadu BJP president following the party's performance in recent assembly polls. He stated a desire for fresh leadership in the state and has been summoned to Delhi by the central party leadership for further discussions.

articles.headline: NCERT textbook shortage bites as city schools reopen
articles.summary: Schools in Bengaluru are facing a severe shortage of NCERT textbooks, particularly for Class 9, as the new academic year begins. A revised syllabus has delayed the printing of physical copies, forcing many students and teachers to rely on digital PDFs and e-books.

articles.headline: India rejects third-party role on border issue with Nepal
articles.summary: India has rejected Nepal PM Balendra Shah’s suggestion for UK intervention in the Kalapani border dispute. The Ministry of External Affairs stated that established bilateral mechanisms are the only way to resolve boundary issues and there is no role for outside mediation.

articles.headline: Kerala boy Tahsin Jamshid to play for Qatar in World Cup
articles.summary: 19-year-old Tahsin Jamshid from Kerala has been named in Qatar's 26-member squad for the FIFA World Cup qualifiers. He becomes the first Indian-origin player to represent the Qatari national team in a major international tournament.

articles.headline: CBSE chief and secretary removed over digital evaluation row
articles.summary: The Centre has removed CBSE chairperson Himanshu Gupta and Secretary Rahul Singh following a controversy over the new digital on-screen marking system. A one-member panel has been appointed to probe the procurement process and alleged technical flaws in the evaluation portal.

articles.headline: WMO warns 80% chance of El Niño during June-August
articles.summary: The World Meteorological Organisation has issued an alert stating an 80% chance of El Niño conditions emerging between June and August. This phenomenon is often linked to weak monsoons and heatwaves in India, raising concerns for the current agricultural season.

articles.headline: Supreme Court says personal liberty secondary to national sovereignty
articles.summary: Deliberating on a case involving national security, the Supreme Court observed that individual personal liberty may have to take a back seat in matters of state sovereignty. The court emphasized that the security and integrity of the nation are paramount considerations.

articles.headline: Karnataka sees sharpest decline in spousal violence in India
articles.summary: Data from NFHS-6 reveals that Karnataka has recorded the sharpest decline in spousal violence among all Indian states. The rate dropped from 44.4% in the 2019-21 survey to 14.1% in the 2023-24 period, reflecting significant progress in social welfare indicators.

articles.headline: Five new Supreme Court judges take oath, full strength reached
articles.summary: Five new judges were sworn into the Supreme Court, bringing the court to its full sanctioned strength of 34 judges for the first time. The new appointees include Justices Surya Kant, S. Chandrashekhar, Aniruddha Bose, Aravind Kumar, and Justice Mohana.

articles.headline: Russia kills 22 in massive drone and missile strike on Ukraine
articles.summary: A large-scale Russian air assault involving 131 drones and missiles targeted several Ukrainian cities, killing at least 22 people. The strikes focused on energy infrastructure and residential areas, marking one of the heaviest bombardments in recent weeks.

articles.headline: US President signs executive order for national security AI vetting
articles.summary: President Joe Biden has signed an executive order requiring developers of advanced AI models to undergo safety tests and share results with the government. The move aims to mitigate national security risks associated with the rapid development of artificial intelligence.

articles.headline: Serena Williams announces return to tennis at age 44
articles.summary: Tennis legend Serena Williams has announced she will return to competitive professional tennis at the age of 44. She plans to focus primarily on doubles events, starting with upcoming tournaments leading toward the US Open, sparking excitement across the sporting world.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court orders removal of actor deepfake videos
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has directed the immediate removal of AI-generated deepfake content featuring actors Varun Dhawan and Naga Chaitanya. The court ruled that the unauthorized use of their personas for commercial or misleading purposes violates personality rights.

articles.headline: Modi to address nation today on Mann Ki Baat
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will address the nation today through his monthly radio programme 'Mann Ki Baat'. This will be the 103rd episode of the programme.

articles.headline: INDIA BLOC to hold meeting today, finalise seat-sharing
articles.summary: The INDIA bloc of opposition parties will hold a meeting today to finalise seat-sharing arrangements for the upcoming general elections. The meeting aims to present a united front.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to hear NEET-UG exam pleas today
articles.summary: The Supreme Court will hear pleas challenging the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET-UG) 2024 exam results and seeking a re-examination. The petitions cite alleged irregularities.

articles.headline: Heatwave continues to grip northwest India
articles.summary: Several parts of northwest India continue to reel under a severe heatwave. Temperatures are significantly above normal, impacting daily life. Health advisories are in place.

articles.headline: Global markets react to US inflation data
articles.summary: Global markets are reacting to the latest US inflation data, which showed a slight moderation. Investors are assessing the potential impact on interest rate policies.

articles.summary: A recent scientific study has highlighted the adverse impact of climate change on marine ecosystems. Rising ocean temperatures and acidification are threatening biodiversity.

articles.headline: RBI likely to hold repo rate steady
articles.summary: Analysts expect the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to maintain the repo rate at its current level in the upcoming monetary policy review. Inflation concerns are likely to be a key factor.

articles.headline: India signs trade deal with Southeast Asian nation
articles.summary: India has signed a new trade agreement with a Southeast Asian country, aiming to boost bilateral commerce and economic ties. Details of the agreement were released.

articles.headline: Local boy wins national chess championship
articles.summary: A young chess prodigy from Delhi has won the national junior chess championship. He displayed remarkable skill throughout the tournament.

articles.summary: The Union Health Ministry has launched a nationwide campaign to raise awareness and prevent vector-borne diseases like dengue and malaria. Public participation is encouraged.

articles.summary: A prominent university has announced a new scholarship program aimed at supporting underprivileged students. The initiative seeks to promote educational equity.

articles.summary: Senior defence officials from India and France held talks in Paris to bolster strategic cooperation. Discussions covered defence procurement and joint exercises.

articles.summary: A breakthrough in cancer research has shown promising results for a new therapeutic approach. Clinical trials indicate a potential for improved patient outcomes.

articles.summary: India's space agency has successfully tested new satellite communication technology. The advancement is expected to improve data transmission capabilities.

articles.summary: Sales of electric vehicles in the automobile sector have seen a significant increase. Government incentives and growing consumer awareness are driving the trend.

articles.summary: A cybersecurity firm has issued a warning about a new online phishing scam targeting users. It advises caution and vigilance against suspicious emails.

articles.summary: The United Nations Secretary-General has urged nations to accelerate global climate action. He highlighted the increasing urgency of addressing climate change impacts.

articles.summary: Indian stock markets are experiencing volatility, influenced by global economic factors and domestic cues. Investors are closely monitoring market trends.

articles.headline: CERT-In Flagged CBSE Portal Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: CERT-In flagged critical vulnerabilities in CBSE's portal for admit cards and results, potentially allowing unauthorized access to student data. CBSE has been advised to fix these issues.

articles.headline: India's WPI Eases to 2.92% in May, Retail Inflation at 25-Month Low
articles.summary: Wholesale Price Index (WPI) inflation eased to 2.92% in May. Retail inflation fell to a 25-month low of 4.25% in May. This indicates softening inflationary pressures.

articles.headline: Coca-Cola Bottler Set to List Next Year for $1B+
articles.summary: HCCB, Coca-Cola's bottling partner in India, plans an IPO next year to raise over $1 billion. This move follows HUL's recent listing plans and aims to unlock value.

articles.headline: IT Firms Binge Shop on M&A for Client Access
articles.summary: Indian IT firms like TCS, Infosys, and Wipro are acquiring companies, especially in Europe and Australia, spending $7 billion in two years to gain access to clients and digital capabilities.

articles.headline: Unilevers Won't Quit India; Investment Here to Grow
articles.summary: HUL CEO Rohit Jawa stated Unilever is committed to India and will continue to invest, refuting rumors of an exit. India is a key market for the company.

articles.headline: Goyal Talks with UK Secy for Trade Signal Push
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal met UK Secretary of State Kemi Badenoch to discuss Free Trade Agreement negotiations. Both sides aim for an early conclusion.

articles.headline: PCB To Sell 51% Stake In DISCOMs for ₹5,000 Crore
articles.summary: Punjab State Power Corporation (PSPCL) plans to sell a 51% stake in its distribution companies (DISCOMs) to private players for ₹5,000 crore.

articles.headline: Sarthak Sidhant Apologises for Class 12 Exams Remark
articles.summary: Sarthak Sidhant, CBSE Secretary, apologized for a remark on Class 12 exam evaluation, clarifying that the board's evaluation processes are robust and fair.

articles.headline: SATs Climate Activist Suman Won't Join CUP Protest
articles.summary: Climate activist Suman stated she would not join the protest by CUP, citing personal reasons and questioning the effectiveness of such demonstrations.

articles.headline: Twisha Death Case: Husband Gets Jail, Mother-in-Law Life Sentence
articles.summary: A court sentenced the husband to 20 years and mother-in-law to life imprisonment for the death of Twisha, a young woman, in a dowry-related case.

articles.headline: CBSE Chief Deepens Row on OSM Recognition
articles.summary: The CBSE chief is under scrutiny for alleged non-recognition of OSM for procurement processes, leading to concerns about transparency and fair practices.

articles.summary: Two members of a regional political party, TVK, were arrested for allegedly sexually assaulting a woman. Police are investigating the case.

articles.headline: Top Court Gets 4 Women Judges After A Gap of More Than Five Years
articles.summary: The Supreme Court now has four women judges for the first time in over five years, with five new judges taking oath. This increases diversity in the judiciary.

articles.headline: Himachal CM Enumerates Cabinet Portfolios
articles.summary: Himachal Pradesh Chief Minister Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu has allocated portfolios to his cabinet ministers, finalizing the council of ministers.

articles.summary: Tamil Nadu BJP President K. Annamalai met with central leaders in Delhi to discuss alliance strategies and preparations for upcoming elections in the state.

articles.summary: Indian water experts indicate that continued unilateral actions by Pakistan, especially after the Uri attack, put the Indus Waters Treaty at risk of being abrogated.

articles.summary: A surrendered Maoist couple in Gadchiroli district of Maharashtra received financial assistance from the government, aiming to rehabilitate them into mainstream society.

articles.summary: An RS MP is reconsidering an investment plan in Punjab due to concerns over geopolitical stability and the state's political climate.

articles.summary: The release of hostages in Manipur was deferred due to "sentimental issues" among communities, highlighting the complexities of the ongoing ethnic conflict.

articles.headline: India Questions EU-Pak Remarks on Kashmir and Silence on Terror
articles.summary: India criticized the EU for linking Kashmir to the situation in Ukraine, and questioned its silence on Pakistan-sponsored terrorism, during a security dialogue.

articles.headline: India Hosts RSP Chief, Rejects China's Party Role in Border Dispute
articles.summary: India hosted the RSP chief while reaffirming its stance against any third-party mediation, including China's, in resolving border disputes with neighbors.

articles.summary: Karnataka Congress chief DK Shivakumar is set to announce a new set of promises ahead of the general elections, focusing on public welfare and development.

articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee called for an all-India opposition bloc meeting to strengthen unity against the BJP ahead of the 2024 elections.

articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is expected to attend the Bengal Foundation Day events on June 20, which coincides with the birth anniversary of Syama Prasad Mookerjee.

articles.summary: Post-election victory, the BJP is highlighting Syama Prasad Mukherjee's legacy and ideals, emphasizing national integration and cultural nationalism.

articles.summary: Priyanka Kharge and Yatindra Siddaramaiah are likely to be inducted into the Karnataka cabinet, signaling generational change and political succession.

articles.summary: The TMC faces internal dissent and leadership criticism, leading to speculation about a potential "Sena-style" split within the party.

articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee alleged that the BJP is using money and threats to engineer a split within her Trinamool Congress party.

articles.summary: Indian companies are struggling to find AI hardware engineers, driving up demand and salaries. This shortage could impede India's AI ambitions.

articles.summary: Indian companies like Infosys are adopting an "agent-operated, human-led" approach to AI, integrating human oversight with AI automation for complex tasks.

articles.summary: PB Fintech (parent of Policybazaar) chairman Yashish Dahiya suggests it may need to become an insurance company, sparking debate on its regulatory status.

articles.summary: Infographic showing the increasing number of data centers globally, with India emerging as a key player.

articles.summary: ONGC secured ₹220 crore from Zoho, Uber, and Paytm as part of digital infrastructure projects, likely related to data center expansion.

articles.summary: SK Hynix plans to double its capacity for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips to meet surging demand from AI applications, addressing a supply crunch.

articles.summary: Skyroot Aerospace projects its revenue to reach ₹977 crore by FY27, driven by increasing demand for private space launches and satellite deployment.

articles.summary: Oravel Stays, parent of Oyo, plans a ₹6,650-crore public offer, reflecting a renewed push after its earlier IPO attempt in 2021 was stalled due to market conditions.

articles.summary: Nifty IT index gained 4% in its best weekly performance in a year, driven by strong quarterly results from TCS and Infosys, signaling positive sentiment for the sector.

articles.summary: South Korea's stock market capitalization has surpassed India's, driven by an AI wave and strong performances from companies like Samsung and SK Hynix.

articles.summary: Microfinance institutions (MFIs) are seeing a revival, particularly in the South, despite some regional restrictions. Business activity is picking up, signaling growth.

articles.summary: Tata Sons chairman N. Chandrasekaran reviewed the progress of defence manufacturing projects, including an advanced materials facility near Bengaluru, emphasizing self-reliance.

articles.headline: Dragon Set to Dig Into Its Oil Stock
articles.summary: Dragon, an oil company, plans to increase its oil stock and potentially reduce imports as global supply chains face disruptions.

articles.headline: Sotheby's to Auction Pelé's Jersey
articles.summary: Sotheby's will auction Pelé's historic 1958 World Cup jersey, worn in the final match, expected to fetch a significant price.

articles.headline: Expanding Offerings: Oil At 1-week High as Mkt Awaits News
articles.summary: Oil prices hit a one-week high as markets await news on Iran's nuclear deal and its potential impact on global oil supply and demand.

articles.headline: Coca-Cola Prepares Sparkling Expansion, George Coffee Too
articles.summary: Coca-Cola is expanding its sparkling beverage offerings and has teamed up with George Coffee for new products, aiming to capture more market share.

articles.headline: Primary Cash-based Transactions to Go Digital
articles.summary: The article discusses the ongoing shift from primary cash-based transactions to digital payment methods, driven by fintech innovations and government initiatives.

articles.headline: A Billionaire Adds Another Trophy Property to His List
articles.summary: A billionaire investor has acquired another luxury property, expanding his real estate portfolio, often seen as a trophy asset.

articles.headline: Jaguar Princess's Revenge Dress Gears Up for Sale
articles.summary: Princess Diana's iconic "revenge dress," worn after her separation from Prince Charles, is reportedly being prepared for auction, expected to fetch a high price.

articles.headline: Anisimova Rises From Ankle Injury to Win at Berlin Open
articles.summary: Amanda Anisimova returned from an ankle injury to win the Berlin Open, showing strong form ahead of Wimbledon.

articles.headline: FootballGPT: AI Unearths Talent Beyond Scouts' Radar
articles.summary: AI-powered platforms like FootballGPT are revolutionizing player scouting, identifying hidden talent by analyzing vast data sets beyond traditional methods.

articles.headline: New York Aim to End 53-Year Title Drought
articles.summary: The New York Knicks are aiming to end their 53-year NBA title drought, with renewed optimism surrounding their current team performance and strategy.

articles.headline: Zverev Brushes Off Jodar To Reach Last Four
articles.summary: Alexander Zverev defeated Jodar to reach the semi-finals of a major tennis tournament, continuing his strong performance.

articles.headline: Unilever Will Step Up Premium Push, Won't Be Late to India Party
articles.summary: Unilever is intensifying its focus on premium products and strengthening its presence in the Indian market, signaling a commitment to growth and market leadership.

articles.headline: Oman Beats Qatar as India's Top LNG Supplier
articles.summary: Oman has replaced Qatar as India's leading LNG supplier, driven by competitive pricing and strategic partnerships, impacting India's energy imports.

articles.headline: Smugglers are Hard at Work as Higher Duty Puts Shine on Gold
articles.summary: Gold smuggling has surged due to higher import duties, making it more profitable for illegal traders and posing challenges for authorities. This impacts revenue and law enforcement efforts.

articles.headline: Telangana Sells Land for ₹2,500 Crore
articles.summary: The Telangana government has sold land parcels worth ₹2,500 crore, indicating robust real estate demand and revenue generation for the state.

articles.headline: Sports Contract Provision Dip for Joster in FY26
articles.summary: Joster's sports contract provisions are projected to dip in FY26, possibly due to changing market dynamics or strategic shifts in its sports media rights acquisitions.

articles.headline: Change in Tune: Audio Streamers Double Down on 'Hybrid' Model
articles.summary: Audio streaming platforms are increasingly adopting a 'hybrid' model, combining subscription and ad-supported services, to maximize revenue and user reach.

articles.headline: Data Centre Demand Sets off a New Wave of Land Deals
articles.summary: The burgeoning demand for data centers is driving a surge in land deals across India, with hyper-scale operators acquiring large parcels for expansion.

articles.headline: Hyundai at 30: Defending Its Turf
articles.summary: Hyundai India celebrates 30 years, reinforcing its commitment to the Indian market amidst intense competition. The company plans new models and EV expansion.

articles.headline: Googly at Google Sparks Hope, Ethics Debate
articles.summary: A Delhi High Court judgment regarding Google's ad policies has sparked debate on ethics and transparency in digital advertising, raising questions about market dominance.

articles.headline: Not Singing in the Rain (Umbrella market)
articles.summary: The umbrella market is facing challenges due to changing weather patterns and consumer preferences, requiring manufacturers to adapt their strategies.

articles.headline: Will ASCI's AI Label Plan Stick?
articles.summary: ASCI's proposal for an 'AI Label' on advertisements generated by artificial intelligence aims to ensure transparency and prevent misinformation, raising questions about feasibility.

articles.headline: Rupee Plays A Higher Note: Growth in Music Royalties
articles.summary: Indian music industry sees significant growth in royalties, with streaming and digital platforms driving revenue, enhancing artists' earnings and attracting global interest.

articles.headline: It Happens Only in India: Classic Ad Campaigns
articles.summary: This article explores the impact and nostalgia of classic Indian advertising campaigns, highlighting their cultural relevance and enduring appeal to consumers.

articles.headline: Rejection Is Just Information: Puneet Gunega Kapoor
articles.summary: Puneet Gunega Kapoor, co-founder of Sikhya Entertainment, discusses the importance of resilience in the film industry and how rejection can be a source of valuable information.

articles.headline: The Danger of Nostalgia: Mysore Sandal Soap
articles.summary: Mysore Sandal Soap, a heritage brand, faces challenges in adapting to modern markets while relying heavily on nostalgia, underscoring the need for innovation.

articles.headline: Shein's Tax Loophole Is Unfair: UK Retailers
articles.summary: UK retailers are calling for the closure of a tax loophole that benefits fast-fashion giant Shein, arguing it creates an unfair competitive advantage.

articles.headline: Guardians of the Brand: Royal Enfield
articles.summary: Royal Enfield maintains its iconic brand identity by focusing on community, authenticity, and design, navigating market shifts while preserving its heritage.

articles.headline: Chinese Luxury Dream Luxury China Faces Frugal Shift
articles.summary: The Chinese luxury market is seeing a shift towards frugality among consumers, impacting global brands as they adapt to changing preferences and economic conditions.

articles.headline: AI Pulls Ad Work Into India Hubs
articles.summary: AI is transforming advertising production, with India emerging as a hub for AI-powered ad creation, leveraging its tech talent and cost efficiencies.

articles.headline: My Name on Your Feed: The Founder-as-brand Boom
articles.summary: The trend of founders becoming personal brands on social media is growing, offering benefits like direct engagement but also carrying risks of low scalability.

articles.headline: House collapses in north-west Delhi; 10 rescued, 1 critical
articles.summary: A one-storey structure collapsed in north-west Delhi's Mukundpur following an LPG cylinder blast. Ten people were rescued, with one in critical condition. The building owner and his brother have been apprehended.

articles.headline: Census enumerators in Greater Noida face FIR for refusing work
articles.summary: Authorities in Greater Noida plan to register FIRs against 29 Census 2027 enumerators for refusing to accept their duties despite repeated communication. Disciplinary action is also threatened against those yet to report for duty.

articles.headline: Saket building owner remanded in 3-day police custody
articles.summary: The owner of a five-storey building in Saket that collapsed recently, killing six people, has been remanded in police custody for three days. The police need to interrogate him to trace other accused persons.

articles.headline: FIR against Delhi AAP chief for 'revealing' rape victim’s identity
articles.summary: The Delhi police have registered an FIR against Aam Aadmi Party's Delhi unit president Saurabh Bharadwaj for allegedly disclosing the identity of a three-year-old rape victim. The incident occurred at a private school in Janakpuri.

articles.headline: Salmonella outbreak linked to imported dried dill
articles.summary: An outbreak of Salmonella Enteritidis infections in the US and Canada has been linked to imported dried dill from Europe. The CDC advises discarding the product and cleaning surfaces.

articles.headline: India’s equity markets slip to seventh in m-cap rankings
articles.summary: India's equity markets dropped to seventh in market capitalization as foreign selling and limited AI stock exposure allowed South Korea to overtake it. South Korean stocks have rallied due to AI chipmakers.

articles.headline: How companies are using workers to train robots
articles.summary: Companies are recording workers' tasks to train humanoid robots, raising concerns about surveillance and consent. Physical AI requires vast behavioural data, with India becoming a key source due to its gig economy. The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, has gaps in addressing AI training data collection and voluntary worker consent issues, leading to debates over commercial value claims.

articles.headline: Supreme Court rules GST on full transaction value for online gaming, casinos
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that Goods and Services Tax (GST) for online gaming platforms and casinos must be calculated on the full transaction value, not just 'gross gaming revenue'. This decision, a blow to the industry, asserts that GST applies to supply transaction value, irrespective of winnings, justifying a higher tax rate for non-essential services.

articles.headline: Fuel and food prices rise in May, impacting inflation
articles.summary: Higher energy costs, influenced by the West Asia war, and increased prices for essential food items like tomatoes, potatoes, and onions, led to rising price pressures in May 2026. This surge is expected to impact the month's inflation reading, contributing to overall economic pressure.

articles.headline: India's fertility rate plunges below replacement levels, alarming states
articles.summary: India's fertility rate has fallen to 1.9, below replacement levels, especially in southern states. This trend is driven by changing societal aspirations, women's education, delayed marriage, and financial considerations. Some states are incentivizing larger families, while the actual replacement rate is higher at 2.2 due to mortality and skewed sex ratios, posing long-term demographic challenges.

articles.headline: India's power demand dips as heat falls, monsoon nears
articles.summary: India's peak power demand has significantly cooled to 242GW from over 260GW due to pre-monsoon showers and receding heatwaves. The monsoon is expected to hit Kerala by 4 June, further easing strain on the power system. Supply shortages have also decreased, though a strong El Niño forecast by the UN warns of potential drier monsoons.

articles.headline: TCS, Infosys, Wipro double Microsoft Copilot licences amid AI push
articles.summary: India's top IT firms, TCS, Infosys, and Wipro, have doubled their Microsoft Copilot licences to 300,000, covering over a quarter of their combined workforce. This reflects a major AI adoption push to transform operations, boost productivity, and drive monetizable AI applications. The move raises questions about the IT outsourcing industry's billing model and hiring practices.

articles.headline: West Asia war, GST cuts hit small auto parts firms; big brands gain
articles.summary: Small auto parts companies face an existential threat due to the West Asia war's supply disruptions and rising costs. GST cuts have narrowed the price gap, accelerating consumer shift towards organized brands better equipped to absorb costs. This trend, impacting helmets, auto fluids, and lubricants, helps larger brands gain market share over unorganized players.

articles.headline: Government to roll out revamped WPI, new PPIs on 15 June
articles.summary: The government will introduce a revised Wholesale Price Index (WPI) with a new 2022-23 base year and new Producer Price Indices (PPIs) on 15 June. This overhaul expands the commodity basket, updates calculation methodology, and aligns India's inflation measurement with global practices. The existing WPI will be published alongside PPIs for five years.

articles.headline: India's deeptech startups attract $1.1 billion funding, VC conviction rises
articles.summary: Indian venture capital firms have increased funding to deeptech startups, with $1.1 billion raised this year, nearly 80% of 2025's total. This indicates growing VC confidence, larger Series A cheques, and a willingness to back earlier-stage ventures, driven by government support, defence spending, AI adoption, and manufacturing incentives.

articles.headline: IndiGo increases owned aircraft to mitigate forex risks
articles.summary: Over a fifth of IndiGo's fleet is now owned or finance-leased, up from 10% in September 2024. This strategy aims to cushion the airline against rupee depreciation, which inflates US dollar-denominated lease payments. The move also includes a stepped-up financial hedging strategy, increasing its currency hedge target from $1 billion to $3 billion.

articles.headline: Mainboard IPOs stall due to West Asia war, SME listings continue
articles.summary: India's mainboard IPO market has significantly slowed, with no listings in May, the first time since March 2025, due to escalating West Asia conflict and global uncertainty. However, the small and medium enterprise (SME) segment remains active, reflecting selective investor appetite for high-growth companies with smaller issue sizes, suggesting a market split.

articles.headline: NHPC OFS subscribed 3.47 times on Day 1, greenshoe option exercised
articles.summary: The government's Offer for Sale (OFS) in NHPC Ltd was oversubscribed 3.47 times on its first day, prompting the Centre to exercise the entire 3% greenshoe option, increasing the stake sale to 6% of NHPC's equity. The offer's floor price was ₹71 per share, aiming to fetch about ₹4,650 crore for the government.

articles.headline: Centre's domestic financing doubles to ₹3.6 trillion in April
articles.summary: The Centre mobilized ₹3.6 trillion through domestic debt financing in April, almost double last year's ₹1.90 trillion. This strong start to the funding program provides a cushion for planned expenditure and insulates public finances from global market volatility. A significant portion came from the National Small Savings Fund (NSSF), indicating strong household savings.

articles.headline: India raises drug, vaccine testing fees to align with rising costs
articles.summary: India has overhauled its fee structure for testing drugs, vaccines, and traditional medicines at government laboratories for the first time in a decade. Charges, in many cases two to four times higher, aim to cover the rising cost of advanced biomedical analysis, strengthen facilities, and support stricter quality oversight across the pharmaceutical sector. New rates take effect in August 2026.

articles.headline: India's fuel exports plunge in May prioritizing domestic needs
articles.summary: India's fuel exports in May tumbled to their lowest level in nearly four years, averaging 878,000 barrels a day. This decline is attributed to a strategic prioritization of domestic supplies during the Iran war shock and refinery maintenance activities, reflecting a focus on national energy security.

articles.headline: India tightens silver import rules, mandates prior authorization
articles.summary: India has restricted silver imports in grain and powder forms, mandating prior valid import authorization from the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT). This measure, effective Tuesday, aims to rein in shipments and ease pressure on the rupee by controlling foreign exchange outflows.

articles.headline: India's May palm oil imports rise modestly, soyoil surges
articles.summary: India’s palm oil imports increased modestly in May from April’s four-month low, reaching 551,000 tonnes. However, refiners increasingly turned to soyoil, which surged 38% month-on-month to a five-month high of 497,000 tonnes, as palm oil’s price advantage narrowed, altering import patterns for edible oils.

articles.headline: Adnoc resumes naphtha exports via Omani port of Sohar
articles.summary: Asia’s naphtha prices fell to their lowest since early March after Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. (Adnoc) resumed exports in May via Oman’s Sohar port. This alternative route could ease supplies impacted by the ongoing West Asia war, providing some stability to the market.

articles.headline: India, China discuss cultural exchanges, tourism ties
articles.summary: India’s consul general in Shanghai, Pratik Mathur, discussed enhancing cultural exchanges and promoting tourism with a Chinese cultural official. This is part of ongoing efforts to strengthen people-to-people ties and India’s growing profile in eastern China, fostering bilateral relations.

articles.headline: Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez to visit India
articles.summary: Venezuela’s interim President Delcy Rodriguez will visit India from 3 to 7 June to hold talks with Prime Minister Narendra Modi. The discussions will focus on energy ties, particularly crude imports, as New Delhi is ramping up its crude purchases from Caracas.

articles.headline: Chinese lab-grown diamonds ride AI rally for chipmaking
articles.summary: China's lab-grown diamonds are benefiting from the AI boom, with demand climbing for their use as chip cooling materials in advanced AI semiconductors. This accelerates their adoption beyond jewelry, enabling denser, more powerful chips. Shares of related firms have rallied significantly, highlighting a shift towards next-generation cooling materials.

articles.headline: Indian startups visit UK to explore AI, tech collaborations
articles.summary: A group of Indian startup founders and leaders are in the UK this week to explore collaborations in AI and technology. The 'UK Startup Safari' aims to move the India-UK innovation corridor from discussion to action, fostering potential for jobs and opportunities in both nations.

articles.headline: Alphabet to raise $80 billion for AI spending
articles.summary: Google parent Alphabet Inc. plans to raise $80 billion through equity offerings, including a $10 billion investment deal with Berkshire Hathaway, to fund its ambitious AI spending. The offering comprises a $40 billion at-the-market program and $30 billion in underwritten offerings of shares and mandatory convertible preferred stock.

articles.headline: Florida sues OpenAI, Altman over ChatGPT safety risks
articles.summary: The state of Florida filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, alleging they knowingly released and marketed ChatGPT while concealing serious risks. The claims include the AI offering instructions to children considering suicide and assisting suspects in plotting crimes, deceiving users about its true dangers and capabilities.

articles.headline: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang advocates paying employees 'as much as possible'
articles.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang stated his belief that employees should be paid 'as much as possible,' weighing in on the global debate about how profits from the AI infrastructure boom should be shared. He made the comments to reporters on the sidelines of the Computex trade show in Taipei.

articles.headline: Excitel Broadband shelves fundraising, relies on internal cash flows
articles.summary: Internet service provider Excitel Broadband has abandoned its plan to raise funds, opting instead to use internal cash flows for growth and expansion. The firm, valued at around $175 million, turned profitable in FY26, reporting a ₹40 crore profit after a ₹74 crore loss the previous year, reducing pressure from investors seeking an exit.

articles.headline: Wipro Consumer Care Ventures expands India, SEA investments
articles.summary: Wipro Consumer Care Ventures plans to increase its investment in funds and startups across India and Southeast Asia, broadening its sector expertise and deal sourcing. The firm will write larger cheques (₹10-30 crore) for pre-Series A, Series A, and Series B rounds, expanding beyond personal and home care to pet care, appliances, and packaged foods.

articles.headline: Vadodara to launch India’s first public blue bond issue
articles.summary: Vadodara Municipal Corporation (VMC) plans to raise up to ₹200 crore by July-August through India's first blue bond, targeting retail investors. Proceeds will fund water management projects. The five-to-seven-year bond will be certified under international norms, with AK Capital Services and Tipsons Group as lead managers, marking a pioneering financial instrument.

articles.headline: Glenmark Pharmaceuticals eyes growth boost in FY27
articles.summary: Glenmark Pharmaceuticals reported Q4FY26 Ebitda up 36% to ₹763 crore, despite missing expectations, aided by lower costs. Management guides for ₹17,000-18,000 crore revenue and 21-22% Ebitda margin in FY27, driven by new product launches in India (Glipiq) and the US (Flovent), and strong performance in Europe and emerging markets.

articles.headline: Patanjali Foods' valuation optimism faces reality check
articles.summary: Patanjali Foods' FMCG revenue grew 2.6% in Q4FY26, driven by biscuit and home/personal care sales. However, edible oils Ebit fell 31% due to price spikes, hurting overall profitability. Analysts question its high valuation given lower Ebitda margins compared to peers and warn that earnings pressure will persist unless price hikes or cost reductions occur.

articles.headline: Analysts back GMR Airports despite West Asia war impact
articles.summary: GMR Airports expects passenger traffic recovery from H2FY27 despite the West Asia war's impact on travel demand and non-aero revenues. Analysts maintain a 'buy' rating, citing tariff tailwinds, airport additions (Bhogapuram operational in Q2FY27), and improving cash flows. The stock is down 8% in 2026, reflecting near-term investor caution.

articles.headline: RBI can remain calm despite brewing economic storm: Expert
articles.summary: An expert argues the RBI can stay calm in its June policy despite rising inflation, growth risks from input costs, and currency pressure. Supply-side inflation from fuel doesn't warrant a rate hike; growth concerns are rising. Bolstering capital inflows via external commercial borrowings or NRE deposits is a better solution for currency, given India's stronger macro stability indicators compared to 2013.

articles.headline: Oyo parent Oravel Stays wins Sebi approval for IPO
articles.summary: Oravel Stays (operating Oyo) has received Sebi approval for its initial public offering (IPO), targeting a valuation of $7-8 billion. The company had confidentially filed its DRHP in December 2025 and aims to submit a public UDRHP-1 by early July. Oyo, now focused on profitability and expansion, has diversified into self-operated hotels and vacation rentals.

articles.headline: Go Colors aims for growth reset with larger stores, broader apparel play
articles.summary: Go Colors founder Gautam Saraogi is shifting from a bottomwear-focused brand to a full-fledged apparel retailer with larger, two-storey stores. This pilot, funded internally, explores everyday-wear including menswear, drawing inspiration from Uniqlo. The move aims to revive growth after two difficult years, as small-format stores struggled with limited product display and declining footfalls.

articles.headline: DevX enters Bengaluru with 111,000 sq ft premium office leases
articles.summary: Managed workspace provider DevX has entered Bengaluru, leasing 111,000 square feet in two Prestige Group properties. This expansion adds over 1,200 seats and is expected to generate ₹2.2 crore in monthly revenue, targeting Global Capability Centres (GCCs) and enterprise occupiers, capitalizing on the city's strong commercial office market.

articles.headline: AI unicorn Netradyne in talks with top Indian GCCs for fleet safety
articles.summary: AI-powered fleet safety unicorn Netradyne is in advanced talks with 6-7 top Indian Global Capability Centres (GCCs) and ride-hailing operators to expand its presence in employee transportation. The US-based company offers AI-based dashcams (Driver•i) that detect risky driving and weather conditions, aiming to diversify its revenue base beyond the US market.

articles.headline: Amazon targets 100 cities for 'Now' quick commerce, leverages 'Fresh' infra
articles.summary: Amazon India plans to expand its 'Amazon Now' quick commerce service to 100 cities with over 1,000 micro-fulfilment centres (MFCs) by leveraging the grocery supply chain built for 'Amazon Fresh'. The two services will coexist, catering to different shopping missions—Now for impulse, Fresh for planned—but share backend infrastructure to accelerate expansion.

articles.headline: Trump pushes to end Lebanon conflict for Iran peace deal
articles.summary: President Trump aimed to quash the growing Israel-Hezbollah conflict, stating both sides agreed to a truce, to prevent derailing peace talks with Iran. He received briefings that the Lebanon fighting was a key reason Iran resisted a deal. The US also bombed Iranian radar sites, while Iran shot down a US drone, keeping the ceasefire fragile amidst ongoing nuclear and sanctions discussions.

articles.headline: US presses neutral Oman to cut ties with Iran
articles.summary: The US is pressuring Oman to sever diplomatic ties with Iran, interpreting Muscat's neutrality in the US-Iran war as hostile. Washington has threatened sanctions and military action, following intelligence suggesting Oman planned to join Iran in tolling vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, a claim Oman denies. Oman's traditional impartiality, a diplomatic asset, is now backfiring.

articles.headline: Russia resupplies Syria air base, retaining strategic foothold
articles.summary: Russia sent a cargo ship to resupply its air base in Syria, indicating its intent to maintain this strategic military foothold despite the Assad regime's overthrow. US officials and satellite images confirm the mission, showing Russia's efforts to retain crucial access for operations in Africa and South America, a concern for some US government officials who hoped the Syrian revolution would deal Russia a strategic setback.

articles.headline: Coca-Cola explores HCCH listing in India, stake sale by 2027
articles.summary: The Coca-Cola Company is exploring a potential public listing of Hindustan Coca-Cola Holdings Pvt. Ltd (HCCH), its Indian bottling arm's parent, on NSE and BSE in 2027, and considering selling a portion of its stake. This move aims to complete HCCH's refranchising and capitalize on the Indian market, where it operates 14 bottling plants.

articles.headline: India, UK discuss sticking points delaying Ceta implementation
articles.summary: India and the UK discussed issues delaying their Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (Ceta). Key sticking points include Britain’s steel safeguard measure and carbon border adjustment mechanism (CBAM). India may rebalance duty concessions on British products like Scotch whisky if these issues are not resolved, as the UK will limit tariff-free steel imports from 1 July.

articles.headline: Government begins search to fill two Sebi whole-time member posts
articles.summary: The government has invited applications to fill two whole-time member posts at Sebi, as the current members, Kamlesh Chandra Varshney and Amarjeet Singh, complete their three-year tenure in September. The tenure is up to five years, subject to a 65-year age limit, with reappointment permissible for eligible candidates.

articles.headline: Government ousts CBSE officials, probes on-screen marking procurement
articles.summary: The government removed two top CBSE officials over alleged irregularities in the Class 12 digital evaluation system and ordered a high-powered committee probe into the procurement of on-screen marking (OSM) services. A student affected by the system also presented anomalies in CBSE’s tendering process to a parliamentary panel, highlighting a national education concern.

articles.headline: Rupee depreciates 17 paise to close at 95.36 against US dollar
articles.summary: The rupee depreciated 17 paise to close at 95.36 against the US dollar on Tuesday. This fall was driven by a jump in global crude oil prices, a firm American currency due to safe-haven inflows, and significant foreign fund outflows. Analysts expect a negative bias on the rupee due to geopolitical tensions and crude prices.

articles.headline: RBI to conduct ₹50,000 crore VRR auction on Wednesday
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) will conduct a two-day variable rate repo (VRR) auction of ₹50,000 crore on Wednesday. This comes as banking system liquidity surplus narrowed to ₹85,411.44 crore as of 1 June. A previous VRR auction saw weak demand from banks despite the liquidity situation.

articles.headline: Mother Dairy eyes 20% growth, new markets this fiscal
articles.summary: Mother Dairy expects a 20% growth in turnover to over ₹24,000 crore this fiscal year, driven by rising consumer demand and plans to enter new markets beyond its Delhi-NCR centric perception. The company, a wholly-owned subsidiary of NDDB, posted ₹20,300 crore turnover in 2025-26, aiming for national expansion.

articles.headline: OTT platforms pair ads with emotions amid content clutter
articles.summary: Streaming platforms are becoming selective about ad placement, aligning ad categories with viewer mindset, emotion, and consumption behavior. This "contextual targeting" has significantly improved click-through rates (CTR) and view rates on platforms like YouTube. Advertisers are moving beyond basic demographics to genre-based, language, region, and device-level targeting, seeking the right viewing environment.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh fast-tracks ambitious quantum mission in Amaravati
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh is fast-tracking an ambitious, infrastructure-first quantum mission in its new capital, Amaravati. Spearheaded by CM N. Chandrababu Naidu, the initiative aims to build a complete ecosystem combining research, skilling, startups, and advanced computing around a 120-plus qubit IBM Quantum System Two. India's National Quantum Mission (₹6,000 crore) targets 1,000 qubits by 2030, but relies on global suppliers for many critical components.

articles.headline: Should small finance banks be part of your FD strategy?
articles.summary: Small finance banks (SFBs) offer higher fixed deposit (FD) rates (5.75-7.90%) and features like penalty-free withdrawals or repo-linked savings accounts, aiming to attract depositors. While deposits up to ₹5 lakh are insured by DICGC, experts caution about SFBs' higher credit risk due to concentrated loan books in microfinance and small business lending. Investors are advised to assess asset allocation and understand that offers can change.

articles.headline: Karnataka HC backs legal aid in insurance ombudsman cases
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that an insurance ombudsman cannot deny legal assistance to complainants at the adjudicatory stage, upholding a fundamental procedural right. This revives debate on policyholders navigating complex, technical disputes unaided against well-resourced insurers. Critics fear delays, but a structured framework allowing assistance, while regulating fees and preserving mediation, is proposed to build trust.

articles.headline: Chinese FDI welcome but vigilance needed on innovation spillbacks
articles.summary: A research paper reveals China's global FDI footprint is $2.1 trillion, broader than official statistics, often routed via tax havens to knowledge-intensive sectors. This private sector-led FDI, driven by China's "Made In China" program, shows acquired firms' R&D spending increases, but patent filings do not. This suggests systematic innovation spillbacks benefit Chinese parent firms, raising vigilance concerns for countries like India.

articles.headline: Powell defends central bank independence; RBI's past efforts noted
articles.summary: Former Fed chair Jerome Powell's defence of central bank independence underscores the need to steer clear of political pressure, ensuring monetary policy serves long-term prosperity over electoral cycles. While India's RBI has made strides towards independence since 1997, particularly under flexible inflation targeting, the nation must continue to insulate it better from political interference.

articles.headline: India's chip ecosystem push is a geo-strategic play
articles.summary: India's drive for a semiconductor ecosystem, highlighted by Tata Electronics' MoU with ASML, is a geo-strategic play. Economically, it reduces import dependence; geopolitically, it diversifies global supply chains from Taiwan/Korea; strategically, it boosts national security in defence, AI, and cyber. The ₹76,000 crore India Semiconductor Mission offers significant fiscal support, complemented by state policies, to build an integrated ecosystem.

articles.headline: Defining 'working class' blurs amid changing US economy
articles.summary: The term 'working class' is losing its clear definition in the US, scrambled by an economy transforming work and compensation. Traditionally manual laborers, often financially precarious, the working class evolved to become a middle-class backbone with union jobs. Now, technology reduces physical and routine jobs, while AI threatens white-collar roles. The 'new' working class may be smaller, better paid, or comprise those relying on benefits.

articles.headline: Pope Leo XIV's encyclical sparks debate on AI's human impact
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV's encyclical calls AI a profound threat to human dignity, questioning its trajectory in warfare, law enforcement, and its automation-first design. He argues technology is not neutral and that current AI, centered on mimicking humans, suboptimally displaces rather than complements human skills. The author urges demanding a different design, proposing antitrust action, human-complementary AI investment, and worker rights over data.

articles.headline: Agentic AI needs deployment records for liability: Expert
articles.summary: As agentic AI systems autonomously coordinate to maximize profits or employ "surveillance pricing," traditional competition laws struggle to attribute intent. The author argues that holding builders liable is unfair, but deploying companies face absolute liability under Indian law. To prevent chilling AI development, a system requiring records of who deployed each agent and its functional limits is needed to assign proportional liability, not absolute responsibility, when harm occurs.

articles.headline: Delhi Tackles Water Crisis with Rationalisation Project
articles.summary: Delhi's water minister announced a project to ensure equal distribution amid rising demand and supply scarcity. The government is implementing infrastructure measures to address water challenges, emphasizing finite resources.

articles.headline: CBSE Chairman and Secretary Replaced Amid Marking Irregularities
articles.summary: The Centre replaced the CBSE chairman and secretary following irregularities in the Class 12 marking system. A probe committee will investigate the on-screen marking platform. Rahul Singh and Himanshu Gupta have been moved out.

articles.headline: Maharashtra Approves Massive Farmer Relief Package
articles.summary: The Maharashtra cabinet approved a ₹30,585 crore package, including farm loan waivers and incentives for timely repayment. This aims to benefit approximately 5.37 million farmers and alleviate agricultural distress.

articles.headline: Venezuela Acting President Alleges US Abduction of President Maduro
articles.summary: During her India visit, Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodriguez alleged the US military abducted President Nicolás Maduro. The US has denied involvement. Rodriguez is in India to discuss energy ties.

articles.headline: ED Raids Vedanta Group Over Alleged FEMA Violations
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate searched Vedanta Group premises in Delhi and Mumbai as part of a probe into alleged Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA) violations, particularly concerning royalty transactions with overseas subsidiaries.

articles.headline: Trump Criticizes Netanyahu Over Lebanon Offensive
articles.summary: President-elect Donald Trump reportedly used strong language against Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu in a private call, criticizing Israel's military strategy in Lebanon. This signals a potential shift in US policy.

articles.headline: Monsoon Onset in Kerala Expected Around June 4
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department predicts the southwest monsoon's arrival in Kerala around June 4. This forecast comes amidst warnings of an 80% chance of El Nino developing, potentially increasing extreme weather risks.

articles.headline: India and US Begin Talks on Interim Trade Deal
articles.summary: Negotiators from India and the US are discussing an interim trade agreement to benefit Indian goods in the US market. Discussions cover tariffs and market access for agricultural and dairy products.

articles.headline: Trump Appoints Loyal Ally as Acting Director of National Intelligence
articles.summary: President Trump appointed Bill Pulte, head of the Federal Housing Finance Agency and a loyalist, as acting director of national intelligence. Pulte has no prior national security experience.

articles.headline: RBI May Exempt Tata Sons from Listing Requirements
articles.summary: The RBI is unlikely to require Tata Sons to list, assessing it poses no systemic risk. This offers relief to the conglomerate, which prefers to remain privately held, avoiding potential market volatility.

articles.headline: Producer Price Index to Replace WPI for Inflation Assessment
articles.summary: India plans to replace the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) with a Producer Price Index (PPI) for a more realistic inflation assessment. The PPI will be introduced from June 15.

articles.headline: India Slips to Seventh in Global Market Cap Ranking
articles.summary: India's equity markets have fallen to seventh in global market capitalization, overtaken by South Korea. Heavy foreign selling and limited AI stock exposure contributed to the decline.

articles.headline: Agilitas Sports Secures ₹225 Crore Funding
articles.summary: Agilitas Sports raised ₹225 crore from Nexus Venture Partners and Rainmatter Finance to expand its athletic wear business across manufacturing, branding, technology, and retail.

articles.headline: Godrej Group Launches Wealth Management Arm
articles.summary: Godrej Investments has launched Godrej Wealth, aiming for ₹1 trillion in assets under management by 2031. The arm will initially offer third-party products to high-net-worth individuals.

articles.headline: Blackstone Closes Record $13.1 Billion Asia-Focused PE Fund
articles.summary: Blackstone's third Asia-focused private equity fund closed at $13.1 billion, exceeding its target. This oversubscribed fund is its largest to date, reflecting strong investor confidence in the region.

articles.headline: D.K. Shivakumar to Be Sworn in as Karnataka CM
articles.summary: D.K. Shivakumar will be sworn in as the 34th Chief Minister of Karnataka, marking the leadership transition. Siddaramaiah will step down.

articles.headline: Maharashtra CM Assures Continuation of 'Ladki Bahin' Scheme
articles.summary: Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis confirmed the 'Mukhyamantri Majhi Ladki Bahin' scheme will continue, benefiting 1.7 crore women. The government also approved a ₹2 lakh farm loan waiver.

articles.headline: PDP Chief Urges United Outreach for J&K
articles.summary: PDP president Mehbooba Mufti called for a 'Ladakh-like united outreach' to the Centre to end the 'stalemate' in Jammu and Kashmir and initiate dialogue.

articles.headline: NIA Charges 31 in West Bengal SIR Cases
articles.summary: The NIA charged 31 individuals in four cases related to road blockades and illegal detention of judicial officers in West Bengal during a special intensive revision of electoral rolls.

articles.headline: Madhya Pradesh Relaunches Public Bus Service
articles.summary: Madhya Pradesh will relaunch its statewide public transport bus service after a 21-year hiatus, with phases before Rakshabandhan. The service was discontinued in 2005.

articles.headline: Australian Teacher Missing in Punjab; Daughter Seeks CM's Help
articles.summary: The daughter of a missing Australian teacher in Punjab has appealed to CM Bhagwant Mann for assistance. Police are investigating the teacher's brother, who is wanted in an NDPS case.

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee Vows to Fight BJP Until Defeat
articles.summary: Former West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee pledged to continue fighting the BJP until its defeat, criticizing their actions against TMC workers and asserting her party's resolve.

articles.headline: Ladakh Civil Society Groups Oppose New Liquor Policy
articles.summary: Civil society groups in Ladakh are protesting the new excise policy to increase retail liquor outlets from two to 20, calling it a threat to the region's social fabric and youth.

articles.headline: HC Seeks UP Govt. Reply on Gram Pradhan Role
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court has asked the UP government for its response on appointing existing gram pradhans as administrators until fresh panchayat elections are held.

articles.headline: Naidu Directs TDP Cadre for Task Force Coordination
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh CM N. Chandrababu Naidu urged TDP cadre to prioritize special intensive revision of electoral rolls for organizational strength and effective coordination.

articles.headline: Activists Allege Forest Rights Act, PESA Implementation Subverted
articles.summary: Tribal rights activists claim a new Chhattisgarh task force undermines statutory bodies and promotes Sangh Parivar outfits in implementing the Forest Rights Act and PESA.

articles.headline: Aadhaar Not Mandatory for School Admission in Kerala
articles.summary: Kerala education officials stated Aadhaar is not mandatory for school admissions but required for benefits like free uniforms. This follows reports of migrant children being denied entry.

articles.headline: Pawan Kalyan Announces Jana Sena to Contest Telangana Elections in 2029
articles.summary: Jana Sena Party chief Pawan Kalyan declared his party will contest the Telangana Assembly elections in 2029, emphasizing his commitment to the state and criticizing the Congress.

articles.headline: CPI(M) Slams Kerala Minister Over Brother-in-Law Appointment
articles.summary: The CPI(M) demanded clarification from Kerala CM and Congress leadership regarding the Electricity Minister's brother-in-law's appointment as additional private secretary, calling it a 'family affair'.

articles.headline: Annamalai Discusses Future Course with BJP Leaders
articles.summary: Former TN BJP chief K. Annamalai met with Amit Shah and other leaders in Delhi to discuss his political future, following dissatisfaction with the party's electoral performance.

articles.headline: India: No Third Parties in Bilateral Matters with Nepal
articles.summary: India stated that existing bilateral mechanisms are sufficient for border issues with Nepal, rejecting any role for third parties after Nepal's PM suggested UK or China involvement.

articles.headline: Congress Criticizes Modi Over Silence on Israel-Lebanon Conflict
articles.summary: The Congress party accused PM Modi of silence on Israel's offensive in Lebanon, claiming it undermines US-Iran agreement efforts and demanding Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation.

articles.headline: DMK Accuses TN CM of Failing Constitutional Morality
articles.summary: DMK leader A. Raja criticized Tamil Nadu CM C. Joseph Vijay for failing constitutional morality, citing an 'aggressive' speech that contrasted with his Assembly address.

articles.headline: INDIA Bloc Meeting Scheduled for June 8 Amid Trinamool Strain
articles.summary: Leaders of the INDIA bloc will meet on June 8 in New Delhi to strategize. Mamata Banerjee is expected to attend, though DMK participation is uncertain due to recent political differences.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi Praises Rajasthan Congress Unity
articles.summary: Rahul Gandhi commended the Rajasthan Congress for its unity, presenting it as a model for other states as the party prepares for upcoming assembly polls.

articles.headline: India Vulnerable to Aircraft Noise Due to Regulatory Gaps
articles.summary: India's aviation regulations overlook chronic aircraft noise's long-term health effects, leading to potential inflammation, stress, and cognitive issues. Monitoring and risk assessment remain limited.

articles.headline: AIIMS Delhi Study: Air Pollutants Harm Foetal Growth
articles.summary: An AIIMS Delhi study reveals urban air pollution silences a key foetal growth protein, leading to low birth weight and preeclampsia. PM2.5 exposure impairs placental function.

articles.headline: Extreme Heat Stresses India's Record Milk Production
articles.summary: India's record milk production is threatened by extreme heat, causing reduced feed intake, lower yields, and reproductive issues in cattle. Farmers are implementing cooling measures.

articles.headline: Congress Criticizes Centre Over Odisha Mining Protests
articles.summary: The Congress criticized the Centre over protests against bauxite mining in Odisha, alleging collusion between Adani's Kalinga Alumina and officials, and calling for the Union Tribal Affairs Minister's concern.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog: India Needs to Bolster Local Chip Manufacturing
articles.summary: A NITI Aayog report urges India to boost local semiconductor manufacturing for mature and advanced chips, highlighting geopolitical risks and the need for sovereign design capabilities and packaging infrastructure.

articles.headline: EV Supply Chain Resilience Crucial for India's Transition
articles.summary: India's EV growth hinges on supply chain resilience and strategic autonomy, not just sales. Import dependency, especially from China, necessitates diversification and a structured 'EV supply chain alliance'.

articles.headline: CBSE Portal Glitches Plague Re-evaluation Applications
articles.summary: Class 12 students faced technical errors applying for re-evaluation on the CBSE portal due to heavy server traffic and cyberattacks. Issues included verification failures and blurred scans.

articles.headline: ABVP Raises Concerns Over Lapses in National Entrance Exams
articles.summary: The ABVP has voiced concerns over administrative failures, security lapses, and inadequate infrastructure in NEET, CUET, and UGC NET exams, stating these issues erode public confidence.

articles.headline: ISL Contract Dispute Leaves 150 Footballers Uncertain
articles.summary: Around 150 Indian Super League players face uncertain futures due to expiring contracts and a dispute between AIFF and clubs over commercial partnerships, creating ambiguity.

articles.headline: Indian Chess Players Shine in Norway Chess Tournament
articles.summary: R. Praggnanandhaa, D. Gukesh, and Divya Deshmukh secured victories in the Norway Chess 2026 tournament. Praggnanandhaa rose to third place.

articles.headline: Veer Chotrani Targets Asian Games Gold and Platinum Events
articles.summary: World No. 40 Veer Chotrani aims for Asian Games singles gold, which offers Olympic qualification. He seeks to compete in Platinum events next season.

articles.headline: Sindhu Advances at Indonesia Open; Others Exit
articles.summary: P.V. Sindhu progressed at the Indonesia Open, while Lakshya Sen, K. Srikanth, and Malvika Bansod were eliminated. Deeksha tested positive for a banned substance.

articles.headline: Sairaj Bahutule Appointed India's Spin-Bowling Coach
articles.summary: Sairaj Bahutule has joined the Indian team as spin-bowling coach for the upcoming Afghanistan Test, overseeing the spin department across formats.

articles.headline: Zverev Reaches French Open Semifinal; Kostyuk Makes History
articles.summary: Alexander Zverev advanced to his fifth French Open semifinal. Marta Kostyuk became the first Ukrainian woman to reach the Roland Garros last four.

articles.headline: US-Iran Negotiations Show Little Sign of Lasting Peace
articles.summary: Despite potential US-Iran talks, lasting peace remains unlikely due to deep distrust, regional opposition, and hardliners. Translating any memorandum into a durable agreement is improbable.

articles.headline: India Losing Ability to Build Own Scientific Instruments
articles.summary: A climate science report warns India's reliance on imported equipment compromises accuracy and scientific credibility. It urges strengthening local instrument manufacturing and developing an indigenous Earth System Model.

articles.headline: Bolides: Exploding Meteors Light Up Skies
articles.summary: An exceptionally bright meteor, termed a 'bolide', exploded over the northeastern U.S. with energy comparable to 300 tonnes of TNT, originating from an asteroid or comet.

articles.headline: Padukone Suggests Gradual Intro for New Badminton Scoring System
articles.summary: Badminton legend Prakash Padukone proposed a gradual introduction of the 3x15 scoring system alongside the current 3x21 format to allow players to adapt without disruption.

articles.headline: India Must Resist China's 'Early Harvest' Boundary Approach
articles.summary: India should reject China's 'early harvest' approach to boundary talks, which prioritizes Sikkim. A comprehensive settlement based on the 2005 agreement is needed for LAC peace.

articles.headline: Russian Attack Kills 18 in Ukraine; Kyiv Seeks More Aid
articles.summary: Russia's massive drone and missile attack killed at least 18 and injured over 100 in Ukraine. Kyiv appealed for more US and European support, citing a Patriot missile shortage.

articles.headline: Kirpan Murder Case Sparks Debate on UK Knife Crime
articles.summary: A murder involving a kirpan has fueled debate on UK policing and knife crime. The judge condemned the act, while Sikh MPs warned against community scapegoating.

articles.headline: Iran's Inflation Hits Post-WWII Highs Amid Economic Pressure
articles.summary: Iran's year-on-year inflation reached 113.8% in May, the highest since WWII, reflecting severe economic strain due to war and US sanctions.

articles.headline: WHO Sees Sharp Drop in Suspected Ebola Cases
articles.summary: The WHO has significantly reduced suspected Ebola cases in central Africa to 116, with 330 confirmed, after clearing many potential cases identified as other diseases.

articles.headline: Pope Appoints First Lay Woman to Head Vatican Communications
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV appointed Maria Montserrat Alvarado, president of EWTN News, as the first lay woman to lead the Vatican's communications department, effective November.

articles.headline: Israel Strikes Kill 11 in Lebanon Post De-escalation
articles.summary: Israeli drone strikes in southern Lebanon killed 11 people, including children, a day after Iran and Hezbollah agreed to de-escalate fighting. The US reportedly backed potential strikes.

articles.headline: RCB Wins Second Consecutive IPL Title
articles.summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru secured their second IPL title by defeating Gujarat Titans. Key players like Rajat Patidar and Virat Kohli contributed to their consistent performance.

articles.headline: BJP Accuses Telangana Congress of Misusing Funds
articles.summary: BJP chief Nitin Nabin alleged the Telangana Congress government is using the state as an 'ATM' for leadership expenses instead of public welfare, highlighting the BJP's role in statehood.

articles.headline: India's LPG Consumption Fell 19% in May
articles.summary: India's LPG consumption decreased by 19% in May year-on-year due to supply disruptions. Petrol and diesel consumption saw slight increases.

articles.headline: Supply Chain Integrity Challenges for India's Circular Economy
articles.summary: Industry leaders noted challenges in formalizing supply chain integrity for circular economy initiatives in India. JSW Steel highlighted using 99.99% of its waste as byproducts.

articles.headline: HUL Inaugurates Unilever Fragrance Hub in Mumbai
articles.summary: Hindustan Unilever Ltd. opened the Unilever Fragrance Hub in Mumbai, underscoring India's importance as a growth market and R&D center for Unilever.

articles.headline: West Asia War to Trim MSME Revenue Growth by 100 bps
articles.summary: Crisil forecasts the West Asia conflict will reduce MSME revenue growth by 100 bps in FY26 due to higher gold prices impacting the gems and jewellery sector.

articles.headline: Supreme Court: Marital Status Cannot Deny Compassionate Appointment
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that marital status is an invalid reason to deny compassionate appointment to a married daughter, deeming such presumptions unconstitutional and gender-based.
